LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF CASSAUNDRA ELLENA'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLTF'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS - THE COURT ADOPTS THE RECOMMENDATION AND REASONING OF PRO TEM JUDGE HUIBONHOA AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED AS TO REQUESTS 28-30. THE SCOPE SHALL BE LIMITED TO DOCUMENTS PROVIDED TO SONOMA AND RELATED TO THE UNDERLYING POLICY. GIVEN THE BROAD SCOPE OF DISCOVERY, THESE REQUESTS REASONABLY COULD LEAD TO THE DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. STANDARD DOES NOT OFFER ANY CLEAR OBJECTIONS TO PRODUCTION OF THESE DOCUMENTS OTHER THAN RELEVANCE AND OVERBREADTH. TO THE EXTENT THERE IS CONFIDENTIAL, PROPRIETARY, OR PRIVATE INFORMATION IN THE REQUESTED DOCUMENTS, THE PROTECTIVE ORDER THE PARTIES ENTER INTO CAN PROTECT THOSE INTERESTS. THE COURT ADDS A PRODUCTION DATE OF NOVEMBER 16, 2012. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO FORM OF ORDER AND SUBMIT TO THE COURT BY THURSDAY, OCTOBER 11, 2012. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. =302/HK

Off Calendar Sep-13-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: bennett cohen, esq., 415.864.3246, for plaintiff cassaundra ellena.
linda lawson, esq., meserve, mumper & hughes, 213.620.0300, for defendant standard insurance company.
anne michelle burr, deputy attorney general, department of justice, 415.703.1403, for defendant commissioner dave jones.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT DAVID JONES' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Ms. Ellena has not sufficiently alleged a violation of a specific mandatory duty. A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving Respondent's exercise of discretion. California Code of Regulations, title 10, section 2218.10(a) provides specific mandatory duties on the insurer, and not Respondent. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.
